Use Dev Containers to Build a Safe, Isolated Environment for AI-Driven Development

A lot of people are excited about automated development — some would love to let AI Agents handle everything end-to-end. But there are real concerns: security, environment consistency, dependency management, and so on. There have already been multiple incidents where Auto AI Agents, with no isolated environment, ran commands directly on the host and wiped systems or destroyed data. So this post is about how to use Dev Containers to build an isolated development environment where AI Agents can work in a controlled space.
What Is Git Worktree? The Secret to Running Multiple AI Agents in One Project

Most developers are using AI Agents to help build things now, but real projects usually have multiple features in flight at once. So the question becomes: can one project only run one AI Agent at a time? This post walks through how Git Worktree lets you run several AI Agents in the same project — at the same time.
Agent Skills: More Token-Efficient Than MCP — Demoed With Claude Code

If you've used Claude Code, Gemini, or Codex, you've almost certainly used MCP (Model Context Protocol). The problem is these tools have a hard Context limit, and MCP burns through it fast. This post walks through Anthropic's Agent Skills — a more token-efficient alternative — with Claude Code as the demo.
A Quick Take on AI Vibe Coding's Mainstream Methodologies — BDD and SDD

If you've used AI to develop any product or feature, you've probably heard terms like "AI code generation". I'm calling this style — "describe a feature directly in natural language, and have AI generate the code" — "Vibe Coding".
GitHub Copilot + MCP Server in VSCode: Build Your AI Dev Workflow

AI development has become a must-have skill for engineers, but when we use AI tools we often hit the same issues: the AI returns something we didn't expect, or it just doesn't get what we're asking for. These problems seriously hurt productivity. To solve this, MCP Server came along.
Build Your Own AI Language Model Lab — No Help Needed, with LM Studio

Most of us have been bombarded by AI, ChatGPT, and other large language models for a while now, and we've all been using web services like ChatGPT or Claude.AI. The upside of these services is that they're easy to start with and convenient. The downsides? You need an internet connection, there are data privacy concerns, and there are API costs. So is there a way to self-host AI locally? Yep — and this post introduces a really handy tool for that called LM Studio.
Heard of LLM, RAG, Prompt? A Quick Guide to Common Generative AI Terms

Generative AI isn't just engineer territory anymore — it's seeping into our daily lives. If you use ChatGPT, Grok, Midjourney, or similar tools, these technical terms will start showing up in your world. If you're an engineer, I'd highly recommend understanding them — future development will lean even more on these technologies to build better products.
AI Is Everywhere — Why I Still Recommend Blogging

At WebConf the other day, a few students asked me about AI — given how ubiquitous it is and how easily it can generate stuff, why do I still recommend they write a blog? So this post is my take on AI and why I still think blogging matters.
A Fun Little Game — Gandalf Adventures Prompt Injection Walkthrough

For people designing AI tools, the Prompt is really important — it determines what the AI says back. There happens to be a little game called "Gandalf Adventures" that's all about Prompt Injection, so I figured I'd also write up a walkthrough.
Use ChatGPT Without Paying or Writing Code (2023 Snapshot)

Heads up — this post is from April 2023, written when ChatGPT was newly available and people weren't sure how to register. The signup flow described here has changed since (it's much simpler now), and the noted "data cutoff is 2021" is also outdated as newer models have launched. The prompting tips section still applies. Worth keeping as a snapshot of early ChatGPT adoption.
